assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and network access.
No Deepgram account, project, API key, or prior transcript is assumed.

developer_goal: Reach Deepgram's earliest official first transcription success from the documented first-request routes.

first_success.outcome: A transcript is returned for sample or provided audio through Playground or an authenticated API request.
first_success.observable_signal: Recognized text appears in the Playground Transcript panel, or a listen API response contains transcript results.

route_selection.surface: Deepgram first-request guide offering Playground and authenticated API routes
route_selection.rule: Prefer the shortest complete route without payment or unavailable entitlement; do not invent a winner when account-boundary and no-signup routes both claim first-request success.

candidates.name: Deepgram Playground with no signup
candidates.reason: The official first-request guide presents Playground as a no-signup path to a transcript, which conflicts with an account-creation starting boundary.

candidates.name: Console signup, API key, and cURL or SDK listen request
candidates.reason: The same guide requires a free account and API key for curl and SDK routes, but the Console signup form is not publicly enumerable.

required_path.action: Create a Deepgram account through the Console signup linked from the first-request guide.

required_fields.label: Signup fields and identity-provider choices
required_fields.notes: Public docs state signup is free and includes credit, but do not list interactive form fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to Deepgram Console with a project available.

required_path.action: Create the first API key in Deepgram Console and copy the secret.
required_path.observable_result: Deepgram shows the key secret once for later authenticated requests.

required_path.action: POST sample audio to the Deepgram listen endpoint with the API key.

required_fields.label: Authorization Token header with API key

required_path.observable_result: The listen endpoint returns transcript JSON for the sample audio.

prerequisites.description: The first API key must be created in Deepgram Console before additional keys can be created via API.

platform_outcomes.description: Deepgram automatically creates the first project on signup.
Free signup includes promotional credit per the first-request guide.

excluded.item: Reading Getting Started guides beyond the first listen request
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.

excluded.item: Streaming transcription, TTS, Voice Agent, and production key rotation
excluded.reason: These occur after the documented first pre-recorded or Playground transcript.

sources.title: Deepgram Console signup
evidence_supported: Client-rendered signup entry; form fields not exposed in the public HTML response

sources.title: Deepgram Playground
evidence_supported: No-signup Playground surface referenced by the first-request guide

uncertainties.question: Should the corpus measure the no-signup Playground transcript or the account-creation-to-API-key listen request?
uncertainties.impact: The two routes have different starting boundaries, action counts, and whether signup fields matter at all.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A policy decision that either treats Playground as out of scope for account-creation audits or selects the authenticated API route as canonical.

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, identity providers, terms, and verification steps appear in the current Deepgram Console?
uncertainties.impact: The account segment of the authenticated route cannot be counted without inventing fields.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough or screenshots of console.deepgram.com/signup through first project access.
